sn22.22:1.2: “Monks, I will teach you the burden, the carrier of the burden, the taking up of the burden, and the casting off of the burden.
sn22.22:1.3: Listen & pay close attention. I will speak.” “As you say, lord,” the monks responded to him. The Blessed One said,
sn22.22:1.5: ‘The five clinging-aggregates,’ it should be said.
sn22.22:1.7: The form clinging-aggregate, the feeling clinging-aggregate, the perception clinging-aggregate, the fabrications clinging-aggregate, the consciousness clinging-aggregate:
sn22.22:1.8: This, monks, is called the burden.
sn22.22:2.1: And which is the carrier of the burden?
sn22.22:2.3: This venerable one with such a name, such a clan-name:
sn22.22:2.4: This is called the carrier of the burden.
sn22.22:3.1: And which is the taking up of the burden?
sn22.22:3.2: The craving that makes for further becoming—accompanied by passion & delight, relishing now here & now there—
sn22.22:3.3: i.e., craving for sensuality, craving for becoming, craving for non-becoming:
sn22.22:3.4: This is called the taking up of the burden.
sn22.22:4.1: And which is the casting off of the burden?
sn22.22:4.2: The remainderless fading & cessation, renunciation, relinquishment, release, & letting go of that very craving:
sn22.22:4.3: This is called the casting off of the burden.”
sn22.22:5.1: That is what the Blessed One said.
sn22.22:5.2: Having said that, the One Well-Gone, the Teacher, said further:
sn22.22:6.1: "A burden indeed are the five aggregates,
sn22.22:6.2: and the carrier of the burden is the person.
sn22.22:6.3: Taking up the burden in the world is stressful.
sn22.22:6.4: Casting off the burden is bliss.
sn22.22:7.1: Having cast off the heavy burden
sn22.22:7.3: pulling up craving, along with its root,
sn22.22:7.4: one is free from hunger, totally unbound.
